toml.command: python3 -c "import tomllib; tomllib.load(open('<file>','rb'))" for each commands/*.toml

agent-frontmatter.command: python3 YAML parse + tool validation for agents/*.md

skill-frontmatter.command: python3 YAML frontmatter parse for skills/*/SKILL.md

nonBlockingIssues.description: Example synthesis report JSON in both agents/scrutiny-validator.md and agents/user-testing-validator.md hardcodes "milestone": "validation" rather than using a <milestone> placeholder.

Section 8.3 sealing trigger condition says 'After a user-testing-validator-<milestone> feature completes successfully' but doesn't explicitly state both validators must pass. Implicit from DAG dependency but could be more explicit.
Override procedure step 2 uses 'status is pending' to identify failed validators, which is ambiguous — pending could mean not-yet-dispatched rather than failed.
Mission selection via updatedAt comparison in mission-override.toml could be slow with many missions compared to filesystem-based approaches.
Dead code in session-context.sh: updatedAt value is read from state.json but both branches use file mtime instead. The else-branch is misleading dead logic.
Redundant coverage gate re-check in planner skill Section 7.7 re-runs the same validation from Section 7.6 without explaining what failure mode the re-check guards against.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add documentation that validation override does NOT change validation-state.json assertion statuses. Failed assertions remain failed; the override only affects validator feature status and milestone sealing. This convention should be visible to future workers and validators.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: SKILL.md line 855 documents this behavior but AGENTS.md does not mention it. The validation-orchestration review flagged this as a convention gap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the skillName-to-agent mapping convention: when a feature's skillName doesn't match any agent in the orchestrator's mapping table (Section 3.4), the orchestrator falls back to mission-worker. The planner should prefer skillNames that map to known agents (mission-worker, code-quality-worker, scrutiny-validator, user-testing-validator).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 3 implementation features used skillName 'extension-builder' which isn't in the orchestrator's mapping table. Workers consistently fell back to base procedures. This is a systemic pattern across all features in the mission.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker couldn't load 'extension-builder' skill via Skill tool at runtime
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a Factory platform runtime issue (skill not found in worker's environment), not a project configuration issue. The skill exists at .factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md but isn't loadable by workers. Outside the scope of this project's validation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker couldn't load 'mission-worker-base' skill via Skill tool at runtime
rejectedObservations.reason: Same as above — Factory platform runtime skill loading issue, not a project issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: No test/typecheck/lint commands in services.yaml for this declarative extension project
rejectedObservations.reason: This is by design — the project has no TypeScript, no test suite, no compiler. Validation is done via jq, python3 tomllib, gemini extensions link, and bash -n. Already documented in AGENTS.md.

rejectedObservations.observation: session-context.sh output markers not documented in GEMINI.md for model parsing
rejectedObservations.reason: The output is handled by Gemini CLI's hook system (SessionStart event) and injected automatically. The model doesn't need to parse markers — the content is provided as context. Low-priority documentation gap, not actionable.
